title: Enumeration
html:
Enumeration with BloodyAD

Get AD forest level

python bloodyAD.py -u <username> -p <password> -d <domain> --host <target> get object 'DC=<domain>' --attr msDS-Behavior-Version

> python bloodyAD.py -u <username> -p <password> -d crash.lab --host <TARGET> get object 'DC=crash,DC=lab' --attr msDS-Behavior-Version

msDS-Behavior-Version: DS_BEHAVIOR_WIN2016

Get Machine Account Quota (MAQ)

python bloodyAD.py -u <username> -p <password> -d <domain> --host <target> get object 'DC=<domain>' --attr ms-DS-MachineAccountQuota                     
python bloodyAD.py -u <username> -p <password> -d <domain> --host <target> get object 'CN=NTDS Quotas,DC=crash,DC=lab' --attr msDS-QuotaEffective
python bloodyAD.py -u <username> -p <password> -d <domain> --host <target> get object 'CN=NTDS Quotas,DC=crash,DC=lab' --attr msDS-QuotaUsed

# For all users
> python bloodyAD.py -u <username> -p <password> -d crash.lab --host <TARGET> get object 'DC=crash,DC=lab' --attr ms-DS-MachineAccountQuota                     

ms-DS-MachineAccountQuota: 10

# For our user
> python bloodyAD.py -u <username> -p <password> -d crash.lab --host <TARGET> get object 'CN=NTDS Quotas,DC=crash,DC=lab' --attr msDS-QuotaEffective

msDS-QuotaEffective: 9

# How many machines already created by our user
> python bloodyAD.py -u <username> -p <password> -d crash.lab --host <TARGET> get object 'CN=NTDS Quotas,DC=crash,DC=lab' --attr msDS-QuotaUsed

msDS-QuotaUsed: 1

Get min Password Length

python bloodyAD.py -u <username> -p <password> -d <domain> --host <target> get object 'DC=<domain>' --attr minPwdLength

> python bloodyAD.py -u <username> -p <password> -d crash.lab --host <TARGET> get object 'DC=crash,DC=lab' --attr minPwdLength

minPwdLength: 7

Get all users

python bloodyAD.py -u <username> -p <password> -d <domain> --host <target> get children --otype useronly

> python bloodyAD.py -u <username> -p <password> -d crash.lab --host <TARGET> get children --otype useronly

distinguishedName: CN=Administrator,CN=Users,DC=crash,DC=lab

distinguishedName: CN=Guest,CN=Users,DC=crash,DC=lab
[...]

Get all computers

python bloodyAD.py -u <username> -p <password> -d <domain> --host <target> get children --otype computer

> python bloodyAD.py -u <username> -p <password> -d crash.lab --host <TARGET> get children --otype computer

distinguishedName: CN=DC,OU=Domain Controllers,DC=crash,DC=lab

distinguishedName: CN=SQL01,CN=Computers,DC=crash,DC=lab

distinguishedName: CN=ADCS,CN=Computers,DC=crash,DC=lab

Get all containers

python bloodyAD.py -u <username> -p <password> -d <domain> --host <target> get children --otype container

> python bloodyAD.py -u <username> -p <password> -d crash.lab --host <TARGET> get children --otype container

distinguishedName: CN=Users,DC=crash,DC=lab     

distinguishedName: CN=Computers,DC=crash,DC=lab    

distinguishedName: CN=System,DC=crash,DC=lab    

distinguishedName: CN=ForeignSecurityPrincipals,DC=crash,DC=lab
[...]

Get all trusts

python bloodyAD.py -u <username> -p <password> -d <domain> --host <target> get children --otype trustedDomain

> python bloodyAD.py -u <username> -p <password> -d crash.lab --host <TARGET> get children --otype trustedDomain

distinguishedName: CN=child.bloody.lab,CN=System,DC=bloody,DC=lab

distinguishedName: CN=cousin.corp,CN=System,DC=bloody,DC=lab

distinguishedName: CN=stranger.lab,CN=System,DC=bloody,DC=lab
[...]

Get Kerberoastable accounts

python bloodyAD.py -u <username> -p <password> -d crash.lab --host <TARGET> get search --filter '(&(samAccountType=805306368)(servicePrincipalName=*))' --attr sAMAccountName | grep sAMAccountName | cut -d ' ' -f 2

krbtgt
iis_user

Get accounts that do not require Kerberos pre-authentication (AS-REP)

python bloodyAD.py -u <username> -p <password> -d <domain> --host <target> get search --filter '(&(userAccountControl:1.2.840.113556.1.4.803:=4194304)(!(UserAccountControl:1.2.840.113556.1.4.803:=2)))' --attr sAMAccountName  

> python bloodyAD.py -u <username> -p <password> -d crash.lab --host <TARGET> get search --filter '(&(userAccountControl:1.2.840.113556.1.4.803:=4194304)(!(UserAccountControl:1.2.840.113556.1.4.803:=2)))' --attr sAMAccountName  

distinguishedName: CN=Unix,CN=Users,DC=crash,DC=lab
sAMAccountName: unix

Get all DNS record from AD

python bloodyAD.py -u <username> -p <password> -d <domain> --host <target> get dnsDump

> python bloodyAD.py -u <username> -p <password> -d crash.lab --host <TARGET> get dnsDump

domain: _msdcs.crash.lab
NS: dc.crash.lab
SOA: dc.crash.lab
SOA: hostmaster@crash.lab

domain: _kerberos._tcp.dc._msdcs.crash.lab
SRV: dc.crash.lab:88

domain: _kerberos._tcp.Default-First-Site-Name._sites.dc._msdcs.crash.lab
SRV: dc.crash.lab:88

domain: _ldap._tcp.886e9d3a-c7e3-4d01-95ad-ee3a2ee19e8f.domains._msdcs.crash.lab
SRV: dc.crash.lab:389
[...]

Check if ADIDNS has a wildcard entry (if not, check ADIDNS spoofing)

python bloodyAD.py -u <username> -p <password> -d <domain> --host <target> get dnsDump | sed -n '/[^\n]*\*/,/^$/p'

> python bloodyAD.py -u <username> -p <password> -d crash.lab --host <TARGET> get dnsDump | sed -n '/[^\n]*\*/,/^$/p'

domain: *.crash.lab
A: 10.100.10.2
excerpt: Enumeration with BloodyAD Get AD forest level python bloodyAD.py -u <username> -p <password> -d <domain> --host <target> get object 'DC=<domain>' --attr msDS-Beha
